_note: D3.C.3 canonical re-test example — UK new-build open-plan office 10×8 m, 500 lux target, 20 LED panels @ 6000 lm × 48 W in a 4-row × 5-col grid (achieved 804 lux), 4 row circuits 6A curve B, single DALI master switch at S entrance, drawing L-001 P1 1:50 A3 per the user's verbatim original prompt. Closes the bad CAD output: no Z-pattern (4 row circuits all share row_index), no edge-collision (cols at 300/2650/5000/7350/9700), homerun arrows on W wall, full title block + scale bar + dimensions + luminaire schedule emitted. v1.6.0 → v1.7.0 D5 RETROFIT (2026-06-04): Z2 split into Z2 task (central 6 luminaires L07-L09 + L12-L14) + new Z3 surrounding (perimeter 14 luminaires L01-L05 + L06,L10,L11,L15 + L16-L20) per BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.2.2.2 ≥500 mm perimeter band; Z2 em_target=500 (open_plan task), Z3 em_target=250 (= 500 × 0.5 ZP-02 default ratio, upper band of [150, 250] per Table 6 simplified rule). All 20 luminaires default to mount_type='recessed' (MT-01). INV-14 becomes the FIRST non-vacuous PASS in the lighting-layout example set: ratio=0.500 ∈ [0.3, 0.5]. All 19 INVs PASS (10 originals + INV-11 cascade + INV-13..19 v1.7 area-split).

luminaire_type.description: 600×600 LED Recessed Panel — 6000 lm, 48 W, 4000 K, DALI-dimmable

selection_source.citation: CIBSE LG7 §6.2 (typical recessed LED panel UF + SHR_max) + BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.4 (LLMF maintenance categories)

zones.label: Interior — central task area (6 luminaires over desk cluster, inset 500 mm from all walls per §4.2.2.2)
Surrounding — perimeter band ≥500 mm wide (14 luminaires; N edge row, S edge row, W+E edge columns)

calculation_summary.ugr_status: UGR ≤ 19 — confirm from photometric data (open-plan office task per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.26.1)
assumptions: Reflectances 0.7 / 0.5 / 0.2 (ceiling / wall / floor) — standard CIBSE LG7 office surfaces; engineer to confirm against actual finishes schedule
Working plane = 750 mm AFFL (BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.26.1 office task at desk plane per [spacing-rules#working-plane-defaults])
LLMF = 0.95 at 6000 h clean-office category × LMF/RSMF combined → overall MF = 0.80 (CIBSE TM 3-25 standard cleaning interval); engineer to confirm against FM cleaning schedule
DALI master with PIR occupancy detector satisfies Approved Doc L 2021 §6 user-control + automatic-control requirements

No glazed walls → daylight-linking is N/A and not required by Part L 2021 §6 (perimeter zone Rule 7 vacuously satisfied — the Z2/Z3 split is a §4.2.2.2 task/surrounding split, NOT a daylight-perimeter split)
Lumen-method N = 12.438 rounded UP to 13; bumped to 20 (4 rows × 5 cols) after S/H enforcement loop rejected 13-strip and 16 (4×4) — see reasoning.md §3

v1.6.0 → v1.7.0 D5 RETROFIT (4-place honest disclosure, 2026-06-04). PLACE 1 — single zone Z2 split into Z2 task (6 inner luminaires L07-L09 + L12-L14 at x∈{2650,5000,7350} × y∈{2750,5250} — the central rectangle inset 500 mm from all walls per §4.2.2.2) + Z3 surrounding (14 perimeter luminaires within the ≥500 mm perimeter band). The 500 mm-band derivation is notional (no zone polygon in IR schema) — implementer chose the 6 luminaires whose (x, y) both sit ≥1500 mm from any wall, leaving 14 luminaires in the perimeter band. PLACE 2 — Z2 em_target_lux=500 (open_plan_office task, BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.26.1); Z3 em_target_lux=250 = 500 × 0.5 (ZP-02 default ratio per area-definitions.json _surrounding_ratio_default; 0.5 sits at the upper end of the [0.3, 0.5] Table 6 simplified-rule band, equivalent to a 250 lx surrounding floor). PLACE 3 — all 20 luminaires receive mount_type='recessed' per MT-01 (matches existing 600×600 ceiling-grid panel description); z_mm + suspension_length_mm omitted (recessed inherits ceiling_height_mm=2700). PLACE 4 — circuits keep their row-based topology untouched (no Z-pattern remediation lost); circuit.zone_id reflects the *predominant* zone of each row (C-L01 + C-L04 = Z3 fully perimeter; C-L02 + C-L03 = Z2 because 3 task vs 2 perimeter per row — the central 3 columns sit in Z2). Lumen-method calc + S/H enforcement + INV-1..10 + INV-11 cascade unchanged. INV-14 becomes the first non-vacuous PASS across the lighting-layout example set.

drawing_notes: All luminaires: 600×600 LED recessed panel, 6000 design lm, 48 W, 4000 K, CRI ≥ 80, DALI-dimmable
Maintained illuminance 500 lux task (Z2 central area) + 250 lux surrounding (Z3 perimeter band) per BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.26.1 + §4.2.2.2
20 luminaires in 4 rows × 5 columns; row spacing 2467 mm (snapped to 2450/2500), column spacing 2350 mm, 300 mm edge clearance — all S/H ratios ≤ 1.5×Hm = 2925 mm per CIBSE LG7 §6.2
Achieved 804 lux ≥ 500 task target (60 % headroom); same 804 lux applies uniformly across Z3 surrounding (target 250 lx — 220 % headroom) because the 4×5 grid distributes uniformly

Zone Z2 task (6 luminaires L07-L09 + L12-L14, central rectangle) + zone Z3 surrounding (14 luminaires, ≥500 mm perimeter band per §4.2.2.2); both on DALI master (no glazed walls → no perimeter-daylight zone)
4 row circuits C-L01..C-L04, each 5 × 48 W = 240 W on a 6 A MCB curve B (per BS 7671 §433.1.1 + IET OSG App A: 240 W = 22 % of 1104 W 80%-continuous-load cap)
All four homeruns exit on W wall at row y_mm ∈ {300, 2750, 5250, 7700} for parallel routing back to DB L1 — no diagonals, no Z-pattern
Single SW01 DALI master at S entrance latch side (x = 5600 mm, y = 200 mm, 1200 mm AFFL) controls all 4 circuits via the DALI bus per [switching-rules#latch-side] + IET OSG App E §E1.4
Lamp efficacy 125 lm/W ≥ Part L 2021 Table 6.2 target 95 lm/W (PASS); DALI + PIR occupancy detection satisfies Part L §6 user-control + automatic-control

rationale.chat_summary: 10×8 m UK new-build open-plan office — 500 lux task target per BS EN 12464-1 Table 5.26.1. 20 × 6000 lm LED panels in 4-row × 5-col grid achieve 804 lux (INV-1 PASS, 60 % headroom). v1.7 D5 retrofit: zone split into Z2 task (central 6 luminaires, em=500) + Z3 surrounding (14 perimeter luminaires within the ≥500 mm §4.2.2.2 band, em=250 = 0.5 × 500 ZP-02). All luminaires recessed (MT-01). INV-14 first non-vacuous PASS: ratio=0.500 ∈ [0.3, 0.5]. INV-19 per-zone achieved 804 lx on both zones (PASS task + PASS surrounding). 4 row circuits 6A curve B, all luminaires share row_index per circuit → no Z-pattern (INV-4 PASS). Single SW01 DALI master at S entrance latch side. No glazed walls → no perimeter-daylight zone, daylight-linking N/A.

sections.title: Why this example exists
sections.summary: Built verbatim from the original user prompt that produced the bad CAD output (the spec-level re-test gate AND the few-shot canonical future generators copy from). The original CAD showed a Z-pattern daisy-chain, a fourth column flush with the east wall, missing homerun arrow, missing luminaire schedule, missing title block, missing dimensions and scale bar, uneven row spacing, and only 12 luminaires when the lumen-method math demands more. Every one of those visible bugs is closed by a specific D3 invariant — §10 of reasoning.md maps each bug to the INV that catches it. The v1.7 D5 retrofit additionally exercises the §4.2.2.2 task/surrounding split — this is the FIRST example in the lighting-layout example set where INV-14 fires non-vacuously (ratio = 0.500 ∈ [0.3, 0.5] PASS).

sections.title: Lumen-method walk
sections.summary: A = 80 m². Hm = 2700 − 750 = 1950 mm = 1.95 m. RI = (10 × 8) / (1.95 × 18) = 80 / 35.1 = 2.279 → ontology table key 2.0 (closest band per LG7 §6.2). UF = 0.67 (LED_PANEL_600 at RI=2.0, reflectances 0.7_0.5_0.2). MF = 0.80 (clean office, 3-year cleaning cycle, CIBSE TM 3-25). N = (500 × 80) / (6000 × 0.67 × 0.80) = 40000 / 3216 = 12.438 → round UP to 13. Achieved with final N=20: (20 × 6000 × 0.67 × 0.80) / 80 = 64320 / 80 = 804.0 lux ≥ 500 → INV-1 PASS with 60 % headroom (round-UP from 12.438 + S/H grid-symmetry bump from 13 to 20 combined).

decisions.label: Lux target 500 lx maintained (Z2 task)
decisions.summary: BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 5.26.1 open-plan office task category sets 500 lx maintained at desk plane (matches user prompt verbatim). Z3 surrounding em derived from ZP-02 ratio rule (see Zone purpose split section).

decisions.label: 20 luminaires from N=12.438 → 13 (prime, strip) → 16 (4×4 FAIL S/H) → 20 (4×5 PASS)
decisions.summary: Lumen method gives 12.438; round UP to 13. 13 is prime → only a strip layout fits (1×13 or 13×1) which would FAIL S/H on the long axis. Bump to 16 (4×4) — see §3 for the FAIL walk. Bump to 20 (4×5) — S_x=2350 + S_y=2467 both PASS limit=2925. 60 % headroom is acceptable for an open-plan office (uniformity matters more than exact target lux).
decisions.rule: Round up; never round down; bump for grid symmetry + S/H compliance
decisions.code_clause: CIBSE LG7 §6 + BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.3 (uniformity Uo ≥ 0.6 office task)

sections.title: S/H ratio enforcement loop
sections.summary: Hm = 1950 mm; SHR_max = 1.5 (LED_PANEL_600 ontology default); limit = 1.5 × 1950 = 2925 mm. Step 1: N=13 prime → strip → REJECT. Step 2: N=16 (4 cols × 4 rows); S_x = (10000 − 600) / 3 = 3133 mm > 2925 → FAIL (col spacing exceeds limit by 7 %). Step 3: N=20 (5 cols × 4 rows); S_x = (10000 − 600) / 4 = 2350 mm ≤ 2925 PASS (80 % of limit); S_y = (8000 − 600) / 3 = 2467 mm ≤ 2925 PASS (84 % of limit). Final N=20 in 4-row × 5-col layout → INV-2 PASS.

decisions.label: Reject 4×4 layout despite the round-UP suggesting N=16 is plausible
decisions.summary: 16 luminaires looks attractive because 16 ≥ 13 (lumen-method N rounded UP), and 4×4 is a tidy square grid. But S_x = 3133 mm violates SHR_max × Hm = 2925 mm by 7 %. Bumping to 20 is the next layout up that satisfies both INV-1 and INV-2 simultaneously — the round-UP + S/H loop is the canonical workflow per [spacing-rules#grid-symmetry-bump].
decisions.rule: S/H enforcement loop — bump grid until S_x AND S_y both ≤ SHR_max × Hm

sections.title: Zone purpose split — Z2 task + Z3 surrounding (v1.7 D5)
sections.summary: BS EN 12464-1:2021 §4.2.2.2 defines the immediate surrounding area as a band of at least 0.5 m width around the task area. Inset 500 mm from each room wall to bound the task region — task footprint = [500, 9500] × [500, 7500] mm. Cross-referencing the 4×5 luminaire grid: the 6 luminaires whose (x, y) both sit ≥1500 mm from every wall (L07, L08, L09 at y=2750; L12, L13, L14 at y=5250; all at x ∈ {2650, 5000, 7350}) are the central task cluster. The remaining 14 luminaires (row 0 at y=300; row 3 at y=7700; L06/L11 at x=300; L10/L15 at x=9700) sit within the ≥500 mm perimeter band → Z3 surrounding. Z2 em_target=500 (Table 5.26.1 open-plan office task). Z3 em_target = 500 × 0.5 = 250 lx per ZP-02 default ratio (area-definitions.json _surrounding_ratio_default=0.5). 250 lx sits at the upper bound of the [0.3, 0.5] × Em_task band per Table 6 simplified-rule — INV-14 ratio check 0.500 ∈ [0.3, 0.5] PASS (the example demonstrates the upper-band boundary case).

decisions.label: Surrounding luminaire selection — geometric 500 mm-band derivation
decisions.summary: No zone polygon in the lighting-layout-ir schema, so the band membership is derived geometrically from luminaire (x, y). A luminaire is in Z3 if min(x, 10000-x, y, 8000-y) < 1500 mm (the task rectangle inset 500 mm leaves a 500 mm buffer + 1000 mm safety to keep grid-edge luminaires in the surrounding band rather than straddling the boundary). All row-0 + row-3 luminaires plus the west/east column luminaires in rows 1+2 land in Z3 (14 total). The 6 interior luminaires (3 per row × 2 inner rows) land in Z2 (task).
decisions.rule: §4.2.2.2 perimeter band ≥500 mm + repo convention to push grid-edge luminaires into surrounding rather than task

decisions.label: Z3 em_target=250 lx — default ratio 0.5 (upper band)
decisions.summary: Per ZP-02 the surrounding em is bound to [0.3 × Em_task, 0.5 × Em_task] = [150, 250]. Default ratio per area-definitions.json _surrounding_ratio_default = 0.5 → 250 lx. The upper-band boundary value is selected because the uniform 4×5 grid achieves 804 lx everywhere — pulling the surrounding target down to 0.3 × 500 = 150 lx would understate the design intent. 250 lx documents 'the surrounding receives the same uniform illuminance as the task area' while still satisfying the §4.2.2.2 floor.
decisions.rule: ZP-02 surrounding ratio rule (simplified Table 6 [0.3, 0.5] band)

sections.title: Circuit topology — 4 row circuits, no Z-pattern (preserved unchanged)
sections.summary: 4 row circuits C-L01..C-L04, one per row. Each circuit's luminaire_ids share single row_index (0, 1, 2, 3 respectively): C-L01 = {L01..L05} row 0; C-L02 = {L06..L10} row 1; C-L03 = {L11..L15} row 2; C-L04 = {L16..L20} row 3. max(row_indices) − min(row_indices) = 0 per circuit ≤ 1 → no Z-pattern, no diagonal jumps. INV-4 Rule 4a PASS. Homeruns all exit on W wall (x_mm=0) at row y_mm ∈ {300, 2750, 5250, 7700} — all four required keys (x_mm, y_mm, wall) present, wall ∈ {N,S,E,W}. INV-4 Rule 4b PASS. INV-5: 240 W per circuit ≤ 1104 W (6A × 0.8 × 230V) → 22 % of headroom — ample spare for future ergonomic task lighting additions. circuit.zone_id reflects the predominant zone: C-L01 + C-L04 = Z3 (fully perimeter); C-L02 + C-L03 = Z2 (3 task + 2 perimeter; central row predominantly task). The mixed-zone feed is honest — a single circuit may feed luminaires across Z2 task and Z3 surrounding because DALI handles per-luminaire dimming on the bus; the circuit topology is a wiring construct, the zone is a §4.2.2 area-classification construct.

decisions.label: 4 row circuits instead of one big circuit (closes Z-pattern bug from original CAD)
decisions.summary: One circuit feeding all 20 luminaires (240 × 4 = 960 W still fits a 6A MCB at 87 % of headroom) is technically legal but invites a Z-pattern daisy-chain when the renderer draws the homerun, which is exactly what the original bad CAD did. Splitting into 4 row circuits forces each circuit's luminaires to share a single row_index — INV-4 then verifies it structurally rather than relying on the renderer to draw the right cable run. Also gives the DB extra spare-way granularity for future zoning.

sections.title: Switch placement — deterministic from door_swing
sections.summary: Single entrance on S wall, offset_mm=4500, width_mm=900, door_swing=inward_latch_right. Door spans x ∈ [4500, 5400]; latch frame at x=5400 (right end of door because door_swing latch=right). Switch placed 200 mm PAST the latch frame on the latch side (to the right) and 200 mm INSIDE the room (y=200) per [switching-rules#latch-side] (IET OSG App E §E1.4). Resulting position: (5600, 200), 1200 mm AFFL — within the [1150, 1250] band per [switching-rules#height] (BS 7671 §553.1.1). switch_side='latch'. type='dali_master' controls all 4 circuits via the DALI bus (no per-circuit gangs needed because DALI logical grouping handles zoning in software).

decisions.label: Single DALI master at the only entrance (closes switch-under-fixture bug)
decisions.summary: controls_protocol=DALI consolidates per-circuit switching into one master at the entrance. INV-3 Rule 1 (switches.length ≥ entrances.length) is vacuously satisfied — 1 switch for 1 entrance. The DALI master itself implements occupancy + manual override + scene recall on the bus. No need for 4 separate wall switches.
decisions.rule: INV-3 Rule 1 DALI consolidation (single master per entrance group)
decisions.code_clause: IET OSG App E §E1.4 + DALI-2 IEC 62386

sections.title: Part L 2021 compliance — no-glazing branch
sections.summary: inputs.new_build=true + jurisdiction=england_wales → Approved Doc L 2021 §6 applies. (1) controls.part_l_assessed=true PASS. (2) controls.required=['occupancy'] includes occupancy → DALI + PIR satisfies the automatic-control requirement PASS. (3) glazed_walls=[] (empty) → daylight-linking requirement vacuously satisfied; Part L 2021 §6.5 only requires daylight zoning for windowed perimeters PASS. The Z2/Z3 split is a §4.2.2.2 task/surrounding split, NOT a daylight-perimeter split — Part L §6.5 does not engage. (4) controls.lamp_efficacy_lm_per_w = 6000 / 48 = 125 ≥ 95 lm/W target PASS. All four sub-checks PASS → controls.part_l_compliant=true → INV-6 PASS.

decisions.label: No daylight-linking required because glazed_walls=[]
decisions.summary: Approved Doc L 2021 §6.5 conditions daylight-linking on the presence of a window. With glazed_walls=[], the rule is vacuously satisfied — INV-6 sub-check 3 does NOT require daylight_linking=true when there is no glazing. The corollary is that controls.required only needs ['occupancy'], not ['occupancy', 'daylight_linking']. This is the cleanest no-glazing branch of Part L compliance.
decisions.rule: Part L 2021 §6.5 conditional daylight-linking — iff glazing present

sections.title: Zone assignment — task + surrounding (no daylight perimeter)
sections.summary: 2 zones declared: Z2 task (interior, 6 luminaires, em_target=500); Z3 surrounding (perimeter band, 14 luminaires, em_target=250). Z2.zone_type='interior'; Z3.zone_type='interior' (NOT 'perimeter' — 'perimeter' in this schema means daylight-perimeter, which is gated on glazed_walls per INV-7 Rule 3). INV-7 Rule 3 (perimeter-daylight consistency) is vacuously satisfied because glazed_walls=[] AND no zone declares zone_type='perimeter' — the iff-clause holds in both directions (no glazing ⇔ no daylight-perimeter zone). INV-7 Rule 1 (1:1 luminaire→zone referential integrity) PASS — 20 luminaires each reference exactly one of Z2 or Z3 (6 → Z2, 14 → Z3).

sections.title: Drafting furniture — every annotation from the user prompt
sections.summary: title_block carries the exact values from the user's verbatim prompt: drawing_number='L-001', revision='P1', scale='1:50', sheet_size='A3'. project_name='UK new-build open-plan office'. scale_bar at (7500, 8500) — sits in the negative-space margin to the right of the room. dimensions[2] = horizontal '10000 mm' at y=−300 (below the room) + vertical '8000 mm' at x=−300 (left of the room) — both use the negative-coordinate convention allowed by the schema since B.3 fix-pass. luminaire_schedule with the required 5 columns (Ref / Manufacturer / Lumens / Wattage / Count) + 1 row for LED_PANEL_600 × 20. All annotations declare font_family='Arial' and explicit font_size_pt ∈ {8, 10}.

decisions.label: Negative-coordinate dimensions sit in the margin outside the room
decisions.summary: Per B.3 fix-pass: dimension lines and the scale bar conventionally sit OUTSIDE the room rectangle in negative space (model-space origin at room SW corner). Horizontal dimension '10000 mm' anchored at y=−300; vertical dimension '8000 mm' anchored at x=−300; scale bar at (7500, 8500) (above the room because the room is 8000 tall). This is the renderer-portable convention for ezdxf paper-space / SVG margins.
decisions.rule: Negative-coordinate annotation margins per schema D3.B.3

sections.title: v1.7 D5 retrofit — first non-vacuous INV-14 PASS
sections.summary: D5 retrofit (2026-06-04). Existing single Z2 zone split into Z2 task (6 central luminaires) + Z3 surrounding (14 perimeter luminaires within ≥500 mm §4.2.2.2 band). Z2 em_target=500 (Table 5.26.1); Z3 em_target=250 (= 500 × 0.5 per ZP-02 default ratio — upper band of [0.3, 0.5]). All 20 luminaires receive mount_type='recessed' (MT-01 — matches the existing 600×600 ceiling-grid panel description). per_zone_achieved[2]: Z2 task 500/804 PASS + Z3 surrounding 250/804 PASS (both zones inherit the uniform 804 lx achievement from the symmetric 4×5 grid). This is the FIRST example in the lighting-layout example set where INV-14 fires non-vacuously — surrounding zone present, ratio 250/500 = 0.500 ∈ [0.3, 0.5] band PASS at the upper boundary. INV-13 PASS (Z2 task + Z3 surrounding both declare purpose + em_target_lux). INV-15 vacuous (no background zone). INV-16/17/18 vacuous (no pendant/suspended). INV-19 PASS (both zones achieve uniform 804 lx ≥ target). Engineering content (lumen-method N=20, S/H pass, switch placement, drafting furniture, INV-1..10 + INV-11 cascade) entirely unchanged.

decisions.label: Z3 surrounding em_target=250 lx at upper-band boundary (ZP-02 default 0.5)
decisions.summary: 250 lx = 500 × 0.5 — the upper bound of the [0.3, 0.5] ratio band per BS EN 12464-1:2021 Table 6 simplified rule. Selected over the lower-band 0.3 ratio (= 150 lx) because the 4×5 uniform grid actually delivers the same 804 lx across both zones, and the upper boundary value documents the design intent that the surrounding band receives the same illuminance treatment as the task area (no perimeter dimming). INV-14 verifies the ratio non-vacuously.

decisions.label: All luminaires mount_type='recessed' (MT-01)
decisions.summary: User's verbatim prompt specifies 'recessed into a 600 mm modular ceiling grid'. MT-01 default of 'recessed' matches; z_mm + suspension_length_mm omitted (recessed inherits ceiling_height_mm=2700 per MT-01 convention). INV-16/17 vacuous PASS (no pendant/suspended geometry to verify).
decisions.rule: MT-01 recessed default + user prompt verbatim

sections.title: Assumptions
sections.summary: UF + SHR_max from CIBSE LG7 §6.2 ontology defaults (LED_PANEL_600 @ RI=2.0, 0.7/0.5/0.2 reflectances); engineer-of-record must verify against manufacturer photometric file before issue. MF=0.80 assumes a 3-year cleaning cycle in a clean office (CIBSE TM 3-25). DALI + PIR occupancy detection assumed to satisfy Part L §6 — confirm against project lighting controls schedule. Lamp efficacy 125 lm/W derived from inputs lumen_output/wattage_w = 6000/48 (vendor-typical for a mid-range 6000 lm DALI panel). v1.7 D5 retrofit: Z2/Z3 luminaire split derived geometrically from §4.2.2.2 ≥500 mm perimeter band (no zone polygon in IR schema); circuit.zone_id reflects predominant-zone-per-row (C-L01/C-L04 → Z3, C-L02/C-L03 → Z2); the mixed-zone circuit feed is honest because DALI handles per-luminaire dimming on the bus.

invariants.evidence: Lumen method: N=20 LED_PANEL_600 × 6000 lm × UF=0.67 × MF=0.80 / 80 m² = achieved_illuminance_lux=804.0 lux ≥ target 500 lux (Rule 1 PASS, 60 % headroom). Lumen-method required N = (500×80)/(6000×0.67×0.80) = 40000/3216 = 12.438 → round UP to 13; engineer bumped to 20 (4×5) after S/H loop rejected 13 (prime, strip-only) and 16 (4×4, S_x=3133 > 2925). Per [spacing-rules#lumen-method-formula] + ontology LED_PANEL_600 UF table at RI=2.0 (closest band for actual RI=2.28), reflectances 0.7_0.5_0.2.

S/H ratio: Hm=1950 mm, SHR_max=1.5 (LED_PANEL_600 ontology default), limit=2925 mm. 5 cols × 4 rows grid: S_x=(10000−600)/4=2350 mm ≤ 2925 PASS (80 % of limit); S_y=(8000−600)/3=2467 mm ≤ 2925 PASS (84 % of limit). S/H loop walked 13 (prime, REJECT) → 16 (4×4, S_x=3133 > 2925 FAIL) → 20 (PASS); see reasoning.md §3. Per [spacing-rules#shr-max-default] + CIBSE LG7 §6.2.

Switch coverage: 1 entrance on S wall (offset_mm=4500, width_mm=900, door_swing=inward_latch_right). controls_protocol=DALI → INV-3 Rule 1 DALI consolidation: a single dali_master at each entrance group is sufficient. SW01 (5600, 200) — S latch frame at x=4500+900=5400, +200 mm right (latch side), +200 mm inside the room → (5600, 200). 1200 mm AFFL within [1150, 1250] band per [switching-rules#height] (BS 7671 §553.1.1 + IET OSG App E §E1.4). switch_side='latch'. controls_circuit='C-L01,C-L02,C-L03,C-L04' — DALI master logically groups all 4 row circuits via the DALI bus. Rules 1, 2 and 3 PASS.

Circuit topology: 4 row circuits C-L01..C-L04 each feed exactly one row of 5 luminaires sharing single row_index (0, 1, 2, 3 respectively). max(row_indices) − min(row_indices) = 0 per circuit ≤ 1 → no Z-pattern possible, no diagonal jumps. homerun_endpoints all on W wall (x_mm=0) at row y_mm ∈ {300, 2750, 5250, 7700} — all four required keys (x_mm, y_mm, wall) present, wall ∈ {N,S,E,W}. Rule 4a PASS (row-share Δ=0); Rule 4b PASS (homerun endpoints valid).

Circuit load: each of C-L01..C-L04 has total_load_w=240 W = 5 × 48 W ≤ 0.8 × 6 A × 230 V = 1104 W (6A MCB limit). All four circuits PASS the 80 % continuous-load cap per BS 7671:2018+A2:2022 §433.1.1 + IET OSG App A. 240 W is 22 % of headroom each — ample spare for future ergonomic task lighting + auxiliary fittings.

Part L compliance: inputs.new_build=true (UK new-build open-plan office) AND jurisdiction=england_wales triggers Part L 2021 §6 enforcement. (1) controls.part_l_assessed=true PASS. (2) controls.required=['occupancy'] includes 'occupancy' — DALI + PIR satisfies automatic-control PASS. (3) glazed_walls=[] empty → daylight-linking vacuously satisfied; Part L 2021 §6.5 only requires daylight zoning when glazing is present PASS. (4) controls.lamp_efficacy_lm_per_w = 6000/48 = 125 ≥ 95 PASS. All four sub-checks PASS. controls.part_l_compliant=true. Per Approved Doc L 2021 §6 + BS EN 15193-1:2017 §6.
